architecture.summary: In-Context Vectors (ICV) is a two-stage inference-only method for adapting LLMs to tasks without fine-tuning. Stage 1 (Task Summary): forward passes on paired demonstration examples (input x_i, target y_i) extract latent states at the final token position across all transformer layers, concatenate them, compute differences (y_i − x_i), and extract the first principal direction (for paired) or gradient of contrastive loss (for unpaired) as the task-encoding ICV vector. Stage 2 (Feature Shifting): during inference on a new query, the ICV is added to latent states at every layer and every token position with a learnable scaling factor α, followed by layer-norm renormalization to preserve activation magnitude. The modified representations steer generation toward the demonstrated task without modifying model weights or adding new parameters.

biblio.title: In-context Vectors: Making In Context Learning More Effective and Controllable Through Latent Space Steering

claims.statement: ICV achieves a reduction in toxicity by 49.81% and 45.31% on Falcon-7b and Llama-7b models respectively compared to baseline without context on ParaDetox language detoxification task
On ParaDetox detoxification, ICV (paired) achieves toxicity of 34.77% for Falcon-7B compared to 73.09% for in-context learning and 52.78% for LoRA fine-tuning

claims.dataset: Grammarly Yahoo Answers Formality Corpus (family and relationships topic)
claims.statement: On formality transfer with Llama-7B, ICV achieves 48.30% formality accuracy compared to 32.96% for in-context learning and 21.99% for LoRA fine-tuning with 5 demonstrations
On sentiment transfer (positive) with Llama-7B, ICV achieves 75.28% positivity accuracy compared to 63.42% for in-context learning and 65.92% for LoRA fine-tuning
On jail-break attack against Vicuna-7B, ICV with α=0.8 achieves 93% attack success rate (ASR) and with α=1.0 reaches 99% ASR compared to 44% for in-context learning and 0% for no-attack baseline

ICV is not constrained by context length and can scale to many demonstration examples that would exceed the context window limit of conventional in-context learning, whereas ICL requires direct inclusion of demonstrations in the prompt
ICV enables task arithmetic: multiple in-context vectors can be combined via vector addition and subtraction to simultaneously follow different types of instructions without retraining

ICV requires access to model internals in order to add the in-context vector to latent states, making it less suitable for closed-source or API-only models compared to standard in-context learning which only requires text prompting
